last_item: Alerts — warn when enabled rules exist but no notification channel is enabled (silent-failure: rules fired into the void with nothing warning the user).
last_result: SHIPPED. Added a conditional warning Callout ('Your rules won't fire') between the channels card and the rules table, gated on hasEnabledRule && !anyChannelEnabled, self-clearing once a channel is enabled. Review panel: 3 SHIP (UX heuristics — 'the most significant usability gap on this page'; Sam; Priya — 'a guardrail, not a nag'). Restraint critic conditionally REJECTED (preferring per-row or enable-time warnings) but conceded the banner is the least-bad option; resolved to SHIP because per-row would N-duplicate the message and enable-time adds a modal wall to a one-click toggle, and there's no per-rule routing so the page-level banner is the right altitude. +3 tests (137 total). Gates green: typecheck, 137 tests, build, go build (outside sandbox). Next top item: Roles & Databases — dropBusy is a single boolean that disables every Delete button during any one drop; scope it to the target being dropped (high/S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
